title: Are US Corporate Profit Margins Too High?
gist: US after-tax non-financial corporate profit margins are holding near record highs (7.6% in Q1 2026 vs a post-1949 peak of 8.2%) despite cost pressures that theory says should squeeze them; AI-driven labor savings explain only part of the resilience.

themes.theme: Resilient US profit margins near record highs

insights.claim: Conventional theory says rising labor costs, stricter US immigration policy, the shift from just-in-time to just-in-case supply chains, and tariff-driven disruption to globalization should all be squeezing US corporate profit margins, yet margins remain remarkably resilient.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: The forces most investors expect to compress margins are not showing up in the data, so bearish margin-mean-reversion trades may be premature.
insights.quote: Conventional economic theory would suggest that rising labor costs, stricter immigration policies, the shift from just-in-time to just-in-case supply chains and tariff-driven disruption to globalization should all be squeezing margins.

insights.claim: US after-tax non-financial corporate profit margins were estimated at 7.6% in the first quarter of 2026, only just below the post-1949 high of 8.2% reached in the second quarter of 2021.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: Margins sitting near a 75-year high frame the key risk for US equities: little room to expand and historically elevated levels that could mean-revert.
insights.quote: In the first quarter of 2026, US after-tax non-financial margins were estimated at 7.6%, only just below the post-1949 high of 8.2% reached in the second quarter of 2021.
insights.locator: US corporate profit margins remain elevated

insights.claim: The second-quarter 2026 US earnings season has begun strongly, according to FactSet data.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: Early Q2 earnings strength reinforces the near-term margin resilience story rather than signaling an imminent rollover.
insights.quote: The second-quarter earnings season has also begun strongly, according to FactSet data.

insights.claim: AI reducing the need for labor is likely part of the explanation for resilient US corporate margins, but probably not the whole story.
insights.speaker: Gavekal Research
insights.so_what: Attributing high margins solely to AI may be a crowded, incomplete narrative; other structural drivers likely matter and are worth identifying before positioning.
insights.quote: Artificial intelligence enthusiasts would argue that this resilience reflects AI reducing the need for labor. That is likely part of the story, but probably not the whole story.
